sourceMovie.title: Cleaner
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Cleaner works as a action movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The action and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Point Blank
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Point Blank (2019) is the kind of film Cleaner fans discover and immediately send to their group chat. The action overlap is real, but it's the tense emotional pull that seals the recommendation.

recommendations.title: Dangerous
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Dangerous shares Cleaner's gift for action — the 2021 film delivers thriller without overstaying its welcome. Consider it required viewing once you've finished rewatching your first pick.

recommendations.title: The Hit List
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Much of what makes Cleaner compelling — the action, the thriller — shows up in The Hit List (2011) in a slightly different form. Both films understand that tense storytelling works best when it trusts the audience.

recommendations.title: Killerman
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The action energy of Cleaner is alive in Killerman (2019), filtered through a different story but the same emotional register. It earns its place on this list with a tense payoff that rivals the original pick.

recommendations.title: Chief of Station
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Chief of Station (2024) operates in the same action as Cleaner, but with its own distinct voice and pacing. The tense payoff will feel satisfyingly familiar without retreading the same ground.

recommendations.title: Aftermath
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Aftermath (2024) belongs to the same conversation as Cleaner — both anchor their action in thriller emotion that doesn't let go. The tense undercurrent connects them more than any plot summary would suggest.

recommendations.title: Sniper: The Last Stand
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Cleaner's action stayed with you, Sniper: The Last Stand (2025) delivers the same sensation through an entirely different story. The thriller through-line is the real common thread between them.

recommendations.title: King of Killers
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Where Cleaner excels in action, King of Killers (2023) doubles down on thriller to comparable effect. The tonal similarities run deep enough that watching both back-to-back feels intentional.

recommendations.title: Hounds of War
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Hounds of War (2024) is the natural next step after Cleaner: same action DNA, different perspective. The tense streak and thriller elevate it well above a simple genre exercise.

recommendations.title: Lights Out
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Fans of Cleaner who respond to its action will find Lights Out (2024) immediately familiar. Both films share a thriller sensibility without ever feeling like carbon copies of each other.

faq.question: What should I watch after Cleaner?
faq.answer: Try Point Blank, Dangerous, The Hit List—each shares DNA with Cleaner while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Cleaner?
faq.answer: Cleaner (2025) is best described as Action, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Cleaner worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.3/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this action lane.
